author: claude-code-agent

patch.value:
## Goal

A package scaffolded with any `pm package init` / `pm extension --init` capability (commands, hooks, search, importers) — and reasonable hand-authored extensions — reliably dispatch their own registered commands from the CLI, with precise lazy activation. This closes [[pm-nacb]] and removes a sharp edge in the "make creating packages as easy as possible" goal.

## Scope

1. **Scaffold declares `activation.commands`.** `buildStarterExtensionScaffoldFiles` emits a manifest `activation.commands` array listing exactly the command paths the starter registers per capability:
   - commands / hooks / search: `["<name> ping"]`
   - importers: `["<name> ping", "<name> items import", "<name> items export"]`
   This mirrors every first-party bundled package (pm-beads, pm-todos, pm-search-advanced, ...) and gives generated packages precise, lazy, correct activation.

2. **Harden the capability activation heuristic** (`extensionNeedsActivationForProbe`, src/cli/main.ts) for extensions that omit `activation.commands`:
   - `search` becomes a non-terminal positive gate (activate when the probe is a built-in search command) so a `search`+`commands` extension falls through to the conservative `commands` activation instead of being shadowed.
   - `importers` is treated as command-bearing (it registers import/export command handlers) and activates conservatively for any command probe, instead of only on a help prefix.
   Pure search providers (no `commands` capability) keep their search-scoped activation — no over-activation. All first-party packages declare `activation.commands`, so they are unaffected on hot paths.

3. **Docs**: document `activation.commands` as the lazy-activation contract in the scaffold README, EXTENSIONS.md, and SDK.md so authors understand and keep it in sync with their registrations.

## Acceptance

- `pm package init ./kanban-board --capability search && install && pm kanban board ping` returns the starter payload (no "Unknown command").
- A pure-importers extension's `<name> import` command dispatches on direct invocation.
- Generated `manifest.json` contains an `activation.commands` array matching the registered command paths for every capability variant.
- `extensionNeedsActivationForProbe` unit cases cover search+commands, pure-search-on-unrelated-command, and importers-direct-invocation.
- An end-to-end integration test installs a scaffold-shaped search extension and dispatches its command.
- 100/100/100/100 coverage and 100% docstrings preserved.
Scaffolded & authored command-bearing extensions reliably activate for their own commands
Make pm package/extension scaffolds declare activation.commands and harden the lazy-activation capability heuristics so search/importers command-bearing extensions dispatch their own commands.

message: Open fix feature for pm-nacb activation gap

patch.value: Shipped: scaffolds declare activation.commands and the lazy-activation capability heuristics no longer skip command-bearing search/importers extensions. Unit (extensionNeedsActivationForProbe cases), scaffold manifest, and end-to-end integration (kanban-board declared + atlas-grid heuristic dispatch) tests added; 100/100/100/100 coverage preserved.
pm package init --capability search|importers commands dispatch; pure-importers and search+commands hand-authored extensions dispatch without declared activation.commands.
Verified end-to-end in temp workspaces: pm kanban board ping, pm tickets sync items import, pm kb show, pm tk import all dispatch. 5003 tests pass at 100% coverage.

Shipped: scaffolds declare activation.commands and the lazy-activation capability heuristics no longer skip command-bearing search/importers extensions. Unit (extensionNeedsActivationForProbe cases), scaffold manifest, and end-to-end integration (kanban-board declared + atlas-grid heuristic dispatch) tests added; 100/100/100/100 coverage preserved.
